english: flat plate, *kor-kór* (*kor kor*) a deep plate, soup-plate.
(spelling?) W. fence of boards or laths.
, or ta-zig-yúl (ta zig yul), Persia, ta-zig (-pa) (ta zig pa) a Persian.
(Pers. ترازو) W. a small pair of scales, goldweights.
v. re (re).

1. ताल the palmyra tree, Borassus flabelliformis (not the date-tree Cs.) B. - 2. In more recent times, and already in Mil., tá-la (tA la) seems to denote the plantain or banana tree, Musa paradisiaca.
Lex. lamp, lantern.
(ta-lai (ta lai) Mong. ocean, sea), the Dalai Lama, v. Huc. II., 155. Köpp. II., 120.
W. the imitative sound of knocking, *tag-tág zer* (*tag tag zer*) there is a knock, *tag-tág ċó-ċe* (*tag tag co ce*) to knock at the door.
through, v. toṅ (tong) and lteṅ (lteng).
n. of a medicinal herb Med.
v. ཊཾ་ཀ་ (TaM ka) ṭaṅ-ka (TaM ka).
Ssk. the present moment Wdk.
Ld. a sort of hard cake or bread, resembling biscuit or rusk.
v. tob-tób (tob tob).
, *tar-tár-ċó-ċe* (*tar tar co ce*) Ld. to smooth (wrinkles or folds in cloth, paper etc.)

, or tál-ma (tal ma), Cs.: 'a moment', Sch.: 'quick, decisive, penetrating'; tál-par (tal par), Cs. also tál-mar (tal mar), 1. instantly, immediately, quickly C., e.g. soṅ (song) go without delay! Lex. - 2. Sch.: completely, quite through, ɣċód-pa (gcod pa) to hew, to cut (quite through), ḃígs-pa ('bigs pa) to bore through, to perforate; also tal ḃígs-pa (tal 'bigs pa).
a tool with holes in it, used by nailers Sch.

1. num. fig.: 39. - 2. Not originally Tibetan, designating 'water'; this word has found its way into Ld., where it however occurs only in *k'á-ti* (*kha ti*) saliva (water of the mouth), and in *ná-ti* (*sna ti*) mucus (running from the nose). - 3. v. spyi (spyi).
(टीका) explanation, commentary.
, (Sch. yti-t'ug (yti thug)) bad, mean, silly Cs.; obstinate, stubborn Schr.
heath-cock Sch.
W. cap, hat (from the Hind. टोपी?).
, Sch.: ti-p'u mjug-riṅ (ti phu mjug ring) pheasant.
Śty., tú-tsa (tu tsa) Sch., anvil.
1. tig-ts'a (tig tsha) zinc Med.; ti-ts'a sér-po (ti tsha ser po) cadmia, calamine (?) Med. - 2. a musical instrument, constructed of metal Sch.
, the snow-peaks around the lake Manasarowar in Mṅaris, which are considered to be the highest and holiest of mountains.

1. also tig-tig (tig tig), Lex. w.e.; Sch.: 'certainty, surety; certain'. In col. language *tig, d́ig, ṭig, ṭ'ig* (*tig, dig, Tig, Thig*), is frq. used for: to be sure! well, well! very right! also as an adj.: nòr-dag tig-tíg (nor dag tig tig) the right, the lawful heir. Cf. *g̀rig* (*grig*); *tig ltá-ċe* (*tig lta ce*), *tig tsam* (*tig tsam*), tig-tsád (tig tshad) v. sub tig (tig). - 2. Sik. the great hornet.
(from तिक्तक the n. of several bitter herbs, e.g. of Gentiana Chirayta) several species of gentian.
Cs., tig-tsé (tig tse) Ld., the ribands which are wound round the felt-gaiters that cover the lower part of the legs.
= ti-ts'a (ti tsha).
Sp. inst. of ta-gir (ta gir).
1. a small cup of brass used esp. in sacrificing. - 2. the sound of metal, *tiṅ zér-ra rag* (*ting zer ra rag*) W. I hear a tinkling.
